critic_raw: The design uses 'in-batch negatives or hard negative mining' for contrastive training, with 'identical text input (ticket title + description vs. article title + body).' But the BM25 baseline also uses 'the same primary metric (Recall@10)' with 'hyperparameters tuned on the validation set.' What BM25 hyperparameters are being tuned? Standard BM25 has k1 and b parameters, but these are typically tuned once for a corpus — not per-query. Is the BM25 baseline being fine-tuned in a principled way?
defender_raw: Round 1: BM25 with Okapi parameters (k1, b) is a standard retrieval baseline with interpretable hyperparameters. Tuning k1 (term frequency saturation) and b (length normalization) on the validation set using Recall@10 is a valid hyperparameter search. The design correctly specifies 'independently tune and train a BM25 keyword baseline on the same training data' and 'both have hyperparameters tuned on the validation set using the same primary metric (Recall@10), ensuring fair comparison.' The BM25 tuning is principled grid search over the k1/b parameter space. This is the standard approach for BM25 optimization.

rounds.critic_followup: BM25 parameters k1 and b are typically corpus-level parameters that don't require 'training data' per se — they're tuned on the retrieval corpus and query distribution. The design says both models are 'trained on the same training data.' For BM25, does 'trained on training data' mean the document frequency statistics are computed from training-period articles only?
rounds.defender_followup: For BM25, 'training' involves computing IDF (inverse document frequency) statistics from the article corpus available during training (months 1-12). At test time, new articles (months 13-18) would be added to the index and their IDF contributions computed. This is standard BM25 deployment — IDF statistics update as the corpus grows. The design's temporal split applies to the ticket-article interaction data used for model selection, not to the document corpus itself. Both models are evaluated against the full article corpus at test time.

adjudication_raw: One concern: BM25 'training' on training data — what does this mean for BM25. Resolved: BM25 uses IDF from training-period documents; new documents added for test evaluation. Verdict: defense_wins.
